metric_value: from corporate specialists to line-of-business and remote users via network computing
notes: Aberdeen frames IBM BI Family's strategic goal: expanding decision support "to the masses" via network computing — exactly the trajectory BI took over subsequent 25 years.
Visual Warehouse designed for 10-100 gigabyte data marts — departmental/line-of-business scale.

metric_value: clustering, classification, prediction, association, sequential patterns, time-sequence
notes: IBM Intelligent Miner supports 6 types of data mining operations — comprehensive for 1997.

metric_value: evolution of Metaphor DIS product
notes: Intelligent Decision Server is the evolution of the Metaphor DIS product — Metaphor acquired by IBM in 1991.

metric_value: async parallel replication with minimal overhead
notes: DataPropagator uses change log monitoring with asynchronous parallel replication — key for zero-impact OLTP-to-warehouse data flow.

metric_value: bundling multiple updates to same data record
notes: DataPropagator performance feature: hotspot reduction bundles multiple updates to same data; plus fan-out, update batching, data reduction before transfer.

metric_value: cost-based global optimizer across heterogeneous databases
notes: DataJoiner performs distributed joins across DB2, IMS, VSAM from multiple suppliers; global optimizer considers network and I/O speeds.

metric_name: Cognos Impromptu role in IBM BI
metric_value: third-party basic querying tool for IDS
notes: Cognos Impromptu cited as third-party querying tool used with IDS for more basic querying (vs IDS for sophisticated OLAP). IBM later acquired Cognos in 2007.

metric_value: drag-and-drop analytical application building blocks
notes: IDS Capsules: packaged OLAP objects that analysts assemble via drag-and-drop. Typical capsules: market penetration, trend forecasting, fraud detection, promotion effectiveness.

metric_value: web browser interface to DB2
notes: Net.data provides web browser access to DB2 for BI Family — key Internet integration for the era.

metric_value: natural growth from querying to sophisticated mining via IBM value-add tools
notes: Aberdeen: "natural end-user growth path from open, easy-to-learn querying to more sophisticated data mining." Confirmed by the trajectory of BI from 1997 to present.

metric_value: expanding to masses via network computing
notes: Aberdeen predicts IBM BI Family will expand decision support to line-of-business and remote users. Correct directional prediction; network computing became web/SaaS BI.

metric_value: breadth of features + Intelligent Decision Server + network computing integration
notes: Aberdeen: IBM BI Family differentiates through breadth, advanced IDS OLAP, and tight network-computing integration vs standalone BI vendors.

metric_name: Data mart vs enterprise warehouse coexistence
metric_value: flexible slicing between data warehouse and data marts
notes: Aberdeen predicts IBM BI offers flexible warehouse/data-mart architecture. This "logical data warehouse" concept became the standard BI architecture.

metric_value: IBM acquired Cognos for ~$5B (January 2008)
notes: IBM acquired Cognos (which had been a BI tool partner/competitor) for approximately $5B in January 2008. Cognos became the core of IBM Analytics, replacing most IBM-branded BI tools.

metric_value: discontinued ~2010; capabilities in SPSS Modeler (IBM acquired SPSS 2009)
notes: IBM discontinued Intelligent Miner approximately 2010. IBM acquired SPSS in 2009 for $1.2B; SPSS Modeler became IBM's data mining/ML platform.

metric_value: active product (IBM Db2 family)
notes: IBM DB2 remains an active product in 2026, now called IBM Db2. One of the longest-lived enterprise database products. Still competitive in mainframe/enterprise environments.

metric_value: discontinued ~2005
notes: IBM Net.data was discontinued approximately 2005 as JSP/servlets became the standard web-database integration approach.

metric_value: self-service BI (Tableau 2003; Power BI 2015; cloud DW) confirmed Aberdeen prediction
notes: Aberdeen's 1997 prediction of BI expanding "to the masses" was confirmed by the rise of self-service BI tools: Tableau (2003), Microsoft Power BI (2015), and cloud data warehouses (Redshift 2012, Snowflake 2014).

metric_value: IBM acquired Cognos for ~$5B (closed Jan 2008)
notes: The third-party Cognos Impromptu cited in this profile became the primary IBM BI platform after IBM's 2008 Cognos acquisition — an ironic reversal of the IBM-as-platform, Cognos-as-peripheral relationship described in 1997.

metric_value: semantic layer translating raw data into business-defined views
notes: Visual Warehouse Business Views provide semantic layer between raw data and business analysis. Concept of semantic/business layer became standard in modern BI (LookML, semantic models, etc.).

metric_value: Editions (time history) + Versions (audit reversion)
notes: Visual Warehouse editions maintain time-series history of business views; versions allow audit reversion to prior data states — prefigures SCD (slowly changing dimensions) in modern DW.

metric_value: push from laptop or pull from central server
notes: DataPropagator supports mobile laptop replication in both push and pull modes — addressing field sales analytics use case.

metric_value: end users invoke DataJoiner from Lotus Approach and Microsoft Access
notes: DataJoiner exposed to end-user tools (Lotus Approach, Microsoft Access) and developer tools (VisualAge, PowerBuilder). Demonstrates early federated data access for business users.
